top_categories.workout_context: 130ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
12,139ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
At Gran Fondo Suisse, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Gran Fondo Suisse's grand Tour-worthy alpine roads and offroad forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 93 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Gran Fondo Suisse's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before July.
12,139ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Gran Fondo Suisse. Train it until it feels boring.
At mile 60 of 93, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Gran Fondo Suisse, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 93 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Low-cadence force work for 12,139ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on grand Tour-worthy alpine roads and offroad. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Gran Fondo Suisse’s 93 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Gran Fondo Suisse with 12,139ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%. high UV at altitude.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Gran Fondo Suisse requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on grand tour-worthy alpine roads and offroad to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on TT Threshold, VO2max, and Over Under to prepare you for 93 miles of grand tour-worthy alpine roads and offroad in Villars-sur-Ollon, Canton of Vaud, Switzerland.
